resposta:
solution: 
```class Solution:
    def findComplement(self, num: int) -> int:
        mask = (1 << (num.bit_length())) - 1
        return num ^ mask
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses bitwise operations to find the complement of the input number. It calculates a mask that has all bits set to 1 up to the bit length of the input number, then uses the XOR operation to flip the bits of the input number. This approach is efficient because it uses constant space and has a time complexity of O(log n), where n is the input number.
